summary: review_only triage: is_secure=False effective_total_issues=2 (raw=3, false_pos=0, duplicates=1)

sast_review.reason: The code uses xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring on untrusted XML input without any protection against XML entity expansion or other XML attacks. The unquote() wrapper does not mitigate this risk.

The XML parsing on line 20 does not guard against uncontrolled entity expansion (XML bomb / Billion Laughs attack). The user-provided XML payload is parsed directly without limits on entity expansion.

Importing xml.etree.ElementTree for XML parsing without using defusedxml or calling defuse_stdlib() leaves the application vulnerable to XML attacks including entity expansion and external entity injection.

raw.summary: Multiple security issues: XML parsing without entity expansion protection (CWE-776), CSRF token verification not implemented, script execution sandbox not implemented, XML parsing using vulnerable ElementTree (CWE-20), and potential code injection via script execution. All three SAST findings are true positives.
